channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
item.description: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.
item.content:encoded:
The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: North Korea says its latest weapons tests included missiles with cluster-bomb warheads
item.description: North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.
item.content:encoded:
North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
item.description: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.
item.content:encoded:
A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Once seen as a war hero, Australian ex-soldier will stand trial for alleged war crimes
item.description: Until a few years ago, Ben Roberts-Smith was one of Australia's most celebrated war heroes. But now, he will stand trial for alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan.
item.content:encoded: Until a few years ago, Ben Roberts-Smith was one of Australia's most celebrated war heroes. But now, he will stand trial for alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Kukolja

item.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
item.description: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
item.description: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from the past.
item.content:encoded: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from the past.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
item.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.
item.content:encoded:
Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race
item.description: The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: A fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire shows cracks as attacks continue across the region
item.description: Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.

(Image credit: Anwar Amro)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
